item.title: Collapse sub-shell jq extractions in create_sprint and mark_complete
item.intent: Sprint state functions pass item JSON as a single --argjson argument instead of forking 5-7 sub-shells to extract individual fields. Saves ~13 lines and 7 process forks.
item.description: create_sprint (44 lines) calls echo item_json | jq 5 separate times to extract intent, description, steps, AC, scope — then passes each as a --argjson to jq -n. Pass item_json as a single --argjson item and reference fields inline. mark_complete (78 lines) calls jq twice to get item_priority and item_category separately from the same backlog file — combine into one call.
steps: Refactor create_sprint: pass item_json as --argjson item, reference .intent/.steps/etc inline in the jq expression
Remove the 5 sub-shell extractions in create_sprint
Refactor mark_complete: combine item_priority and item_category into one jq call returning both
Verify: sprint.json and sprints.jsonl archive records are identical to before
acceptanceCriteria: create_sprint has zero echo item_json | jq sub-shell calls
create_sprint uses --argjson item to pass the full item
mark_complete uses one jq call for priority+category, not two
